prose:
Frontier-orbital energies of water

Method provenance
Property: HOMO–LUMO energy gap (Koopmans-style orbital energies, eV)
Level of theory: B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted DFT in PySCF (tier "standard")
Geometry: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates (file: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/water/water.xyz); single-point calculation – no further optimization (DFT//input geometry)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software/backend: PySCF (invoked through chemkit frontier-orbitals skill)
SCF convergence: converged in 7 cycles; gradient and density-fitting defaults employed. No warnings were reported by the engine.
Live log & artifacts: the run streamed stdout/stderr to the autogenerated .out file in the working directory (path echoed at launch) and wrote the structured result to /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpl4gkss7d/live.json (see cli_invocation).

Result
HOMO energy   = −8.6037183 eV
LUMO energy   =  +0.33695037 eV
HOMO–LUMO gap =  8.9406687 eV  (headline value)

The calculation used density-functional theory with the B3LYP functional and def2-TZVP basis set (tier “standard”), run in the gas phase. The integrity check reports trustworthy: true.
